content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of length NNN.

You can do the following operation on the array **atmost once**:


Choose any **non-negative** integer XXX and a subarray [L,R][L, R][L,R] (1≤L≤R≤N)(1 \leq L \leq R \leq N)(1≤L≤R≤N) and update Ai=Ai&XA_i = A_i \& XAi​=Ai​&X for all L≤i≤RL \leq i \leq RL≤i≤R. Here &\&& denotes bitwise AND operation.
Find the **maximum** bitwise XOR of **all** the elements of the updated array you can achieve.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
First line of each test case contains an integer NNN - denoting the length of array AAA.
Second line contains NNN integers A1,A2,…ANA_1, A_2, \dots A_NA1​,A2​,…AN​ - denoting the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output the **maximum** XOR of all the elements of the updated array you can achieve.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases do not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111: We can choose the subarray [L,R][L,R][L,R] as [2,4][2,4][2,4] and X=0X=0X=0. Thus, A2=3&0=0,A3=3&0=0,A_2 = 3\&0 = 0, A_3 = 3\&0 = 0,A2​=3&0=0,A3​=3&0=0, and A4=3&0=0A_4 = 3\&0 = 0A4​=3&0=0. The updated array is [3,0,0,0][3,0,0,0][3,0,0,0]. The bitwise XOR of all the elements of the updated array is 3⊕0⊕0⊕0=33\oplus 0\oplus 0\oplus 0 = 33⊕0⊕0⊕0=3.
It can be shown that the bitwise XOR of the updated array cannot exceed 333 after applying at most one operation.
Test Case 222: We can choose the subarray [L,R][L,R][L,R] as [1,2][1,2][1,2] and X=3X=3X=3. Thus, A1=1&3=1A_1 = 1\&3 = 1A1​=1&3=1 and A2=6&3=2A_2 = 6\&3 = 2A2​=6&3=2. The updated array is [1,2,4][1, 2, 4][1,2,4]. The bitwise XOR of all the elements of the updated array is 1⊕2⊕4=71\oplus 2\oplus 4 = 71⊕2⊕4=7.
It can be shown that the bitwise XOR of the updated array cannot exceed 777 after applying at most one operation.
